channel.title: New local problems on FixMyStreet
channel.description: The latest local problems reported by users

item.title: 40 Chesterfield Grove-Flytipping, 6th May
item.description:
A potty is put on the pavement causing blocked path and not appropriately disposed. It needs to be removed.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 40, Chesterfield Grove, East Dulwich, Greater London

item.description:
A large pothole in the middle of the road near a junction with Dobell Road since last winter.

item.description:
Growing litter at the end of Ashbourne Grove near the Vet. It needs to be removed.

item.description:
Furniture has not been disposed of appropriately by the silver gates. This area keeps being flytipped at this specific spot. It needs to stop.

item.description:
Abandoned bike frame under railway tunnel off Angel Lane

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Angel Lane, Elephant and Castle, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Grass on junior football pitch dangerously long – pitch unplayable for children’s matches, 6th May
item.description:
The grass on Harvington’s 9 a side football pitch has become excessively long and was in a very poor condition during children’s matches played last Sunday. The surface was so overgrown that the ball could barely roll properly, making it extremely difficult for the children to play safely or enjoy the game. This pitch is used regularly by local grassroots youth football teams and supports a large number of children and families in the community each weekend. At its current length, the grass is affecting both the safety and quality of play, with increased risk of trips, awkward movement and potential injury. With more fixtures scheduled this coming weekend, we would be very grateful if the pitch could be cut as a priority before matches take place. The current condition risks games being heavily disrupted or potentially unplayable for the children. Thank you for your help and support in maintaining these important community sports facilities.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): South Eden Park Road, Eden Park, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Abandoned bicycle, 6th May
item.description:
A rusty bicycle with a large white triangular advertising board attached is locked to a black bike stand on the pavement. The board reads 'MAN & VAN', '07846539110', and '£50 p/h'. The bike appears old and abandoned.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Arch 54, South Lambeth Road, Vauxhall, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Metal pole protruding from ground, 6th May
item.description:
Someone has fixed this pole in the ground. It's a hazard and needs to be removed.

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Philimore Close, Plumstead
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Waste from the litter bin strewn across the pavement, 6th May
item.description:
The blue sack seen in the picture was taken from the litter and left overnight. Foxes appear to have accessed it and left the mess as can be seen. Can it be cleared pleased because its right outside a school. This is not the first time this has happened

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Hollydale Road, Nunhead, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Constant litter and broken glass at 7 Sudrey street, 6th May
item.description:
There is constant rubbish being left on the pavement directly outside 4-7 Sudrey Street. The rubbish has been there for over a week and a half and has not been collected. There is also broken glass and loose items on the pavement, which is dangerous for pedestrians, residents, children, pets, and anyone entering or leaving the building. Please can the council arrange for this rubbish, including the glass and larger items, to be collected and the area cleared as soon as possible.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Sudrey Street, The Borough, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Parked wrong side of road, 6th May
item.description:
Parked facing on coming traffic, no tax or MOT on this vehicle
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Kent House Lane, New Beckenham, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Graffiti on a number of the utility boxes., 6th May
item.description:
Please remove it.
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 1, South Eden Park Road, Beckenham, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: 65 Munster square, 6th May
item.description:
Thames water have been working here. They have not fixed the manhole so that it is level. It is raised and could be a trip hazard. Near to Laxton Place corner of Munster square where a estate sign is for Regent’s Park estate.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 2, Laxton Place, Fitzrovia, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Unrestricted access to the communal passageway is causing ongoing anti-social behaviour and vandalism; a lockable gate is needed to restrict entry to residents, and the damaged wall requires cleaning and repainting., 6th May
item.description:
The core issue is unrestricted access to the communal passageway between the terraced houses. Currently, anyone can enter the space, including non-residents, which has led to ongoing anti-social behaviour such as noise, littering, and repeated vandalism of the white wall using spray paint, markers, chalk, and other materials. While vandalism is visible, it is a symptom rather than the root cause. The primary problem is the lack of controlled access. A lockable gate is required to ensure that only authorised residents can enter this area. A similar solution has already been implemented successfully at a nearby passage, yet this location remains unsecured. In addition to resolving the access issue, the existing damage must be addressed. The wall has already been heavily defaced and requires proper cleaning and repainting. Expecting accountability from those responsible is not realistic, as the individuals involved are often underaged and their parents not known. Therefore, preventative measures and remediation are necessary. Installing a secure gate and restoring the wall would significantly improve safety, reduce anti-social behaviour, and maintain the condition of the shared space.

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Prentiss Court, Charlton
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Litter sholden gardens, 6th May
item.description:
No proper litter pick or removal or sweeping of litter and rubbish for months

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 5A, Rookery Gardens, Poverest, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Shopping trolley and litter, 6th May
item.description:
Shopping trolley and litter everywhere as usual ,outside and opposite westbere house

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Sholden Gardens, Poverest, Greater London
